name: Poza de la Sal and Valle de las Caderechas Loop
name_original: Valle de las Caderechas y vistas al Valle de Bureba — circular desde Poza de la Sal
description: This difficult 37.7-mile road cycling route from Poza de la Sal offers panoramic views and passes through fruit orchards in Valle de las Cad
region_intro: Road cycling around Poza De La Sal features routes through varied terrain, including river valleys and significant climbs. The region is characterized by its rolling hills and the presence of historic towns, offering a mix of natural landscapes and cultural points of interest. Road cyclists can expect routes that traverse both open countryside and more sheltered valley sections. The area provides opportunities for challenging ascents and scenic descents. Best road cycling routes around Poza De La Sal The most popular road cycling route is Poza de la Sal and Valle de las Caderechas Loop, a 37.7 miles (60.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 1 minute to complete. This route features a significant elevation gain through the Valle de las Caderechas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Alto de los Escóbados from Poza de la Sal — circular through Valle de Sedano, a difficult 41.5 miles (66.7 km) path. This route includes a challenging climb to Alto de los Escóbados and traverses the Valle de Sedano. Local road cyclists also love the Oña Historic Town Center – Portillo de Busto loop from Cantabrana, a 42.0 miles (67.6 km) trail leading through the historic town of Oña, often completed in about 3 hours 12 minutes. Road cycling around Poza De La Sal is defined by valley roads, challenging hills, and routes connecting historic villages. The network offers options for moderate to difficult ability levels. The routes in Poza De La Sal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Poza De La Sal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Poza De La Sal?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes documented on komoot for the Poza De La Sal region. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult, offering diverse experiences through river valleys, historic towns, and challenging climbs.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Poza De La Sal?
A: The road cycling routes around Poza De La Sal are predominantly moderate to difficult. While there is one easy route, the majority (14) are moderate, and 9 are considered difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and longer distances.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Poza De La Sal?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. However, most routes in the Poza De La Sal region are classified as moderate to difficult, often involving rolling hills and challenging ascents. Beginners should look for routes specifically marked as 'easy' on komoot.

Q: Can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs in the Poza De La Sal region?
A: Absolutely. The Poza De La Sal region is known for its challenging climbs. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208884277/alto-de-los-escobados-from-poza-de-la-sal-circular-through-valle-de-sedano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de los Escóbados from Poza de la Sal — circular through Valle de Sedano&lt;/a&gt; feature over 1000 meters of elevation gain, traversing the Valle de Sedano and offering a demanding experience for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there road cycling routes that form a loop or circular path around Poza De La Sal?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the area are designed as loops. For instance, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1061619724/poza-de-la-sal-and-valle-de-las-caderechas-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de la Sal and Valle de las Caderechas Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 37.7-mile circular trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Town Center – Portillo de Busto loop from Cantabrana&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent circular option.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on road cycling routes in Poza De La Sal?
A: Road cycling in Poza De La Sal offers a mix of natural landscapes and cultural sights. You'll traverse river valleys, rolling hills, and open countryside, often passing through historic towns and villages. The region is characterized by its varied terrain, providing scenic descents and panoramic views.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant points of interest. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Center&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4968842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;MonasterioDeSanSalvador&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, some routes might offer glimpses of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4405981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Rock Valley&lt;/a&gt; or even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6676863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CascadaDeLaHuevera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Poza De La Sal?
A: The road cycling routes in Poza De La Sal are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the challenging climbs, and the opportunity to explore historic villages and scenic valleys.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking longer distances, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31902567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de Sobrón Road – Frías Bridge loop from Cantabrana&lt;/a&gt; cover over 100 kilometers (62 miles) with substantial elevation gain, offering a full day's challenge through diverse landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Poza De La Sal?
A: While specific conditions can vary, generally spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for road cycling in Poza De La Sal, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. These seasons provide comfortable riding weather and vibrant scenery.

Q: Are there any road cycling routes that connect to other cycling paths or greenways?
A: Yes, the region features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4988248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander–Mediterranean Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, which can be accessed from some areas. This offers opportunities to extend your ride or connect to a dedicated cycle path for a different experience.
tips: Las Caderechas is a small and surprising enclave in Burgos located on the northwestern side of the La Bureba region. Its broken and isolated landscape, painted with the green of the forests and fruit trees, appears dotted with picturesque towns that have managed to preserve all the flavor of their beautiful and functional popular architecture.
Mountain pass that is located just after the center of Hozabejas, a few kilometers from it.
Near the top of the mountain pass is the Mirador de la Bureba, which offers impressive views of the area. To get there, however, it's time to get off the bike and hit a track.

From the top of the mountain where the castle is located, you can see the whole of the population. The town of Poza de la Sal is perched on the side of a mountain range, fortified and protected by the Rojas castle and with views over the plains of the La Bureba region.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling route is approximately 60.6 kilometers long with significant elevation changes. It typically takes around 3 hours and 1 minute to complete, assuming a good physical condition.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is officially categorized as a difficult road route. The tracks are mostly firm and paved, suitable for road bikes. However, given the 'difficult' classification in this region, some sections might have less than perfectly smooth asphalt. The area features a 'broken and isolated landscape' with varied topography and considerable climbs.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner cyclists?
faqs.answer: No, this route is officially classified as 'difficult' and requires a very good physical condition due to its significant elevation gain and length. It is best suited for experienced cyclists looking for a challenging ride.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle the Valle de las Caderechas?
faqs.answer: The Valle de las Caderechas is particularly stunning during the cherry blossom season, when the valley bursts into an 'explosion of white color.' Spring and early summer would offer beautiful scenery with fruit trees in bloom or bearing fruit. Autumn would also be picturesque with changing foliage.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Valle de las Caderechas and views of Valle de Bureba route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting from Poza de la Sal. You can typically find parking within or near the town of Poza de la Sal to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What notable sights or viewpoints will I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a harmonious blend of wooded areas, fertile valleys, and traditional villages. You'll cycle through the picturesque Valle de las Caderechas, known for its fruit orchards. From elevated points like Alto de los Escóbados and Altotero Pass, you can enjoy expansive panoramic views of the valley and the wider Bureba region, including Views of Poza de la Sal.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling. While there are no specific restrictions mentioned regarding dogs, it's generally not advisable to bring dogs on a challenging road cycling route of this nature due to safety concerns for both the animal and the cyclist.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will find yourself on parts of the Camino de Las Merindades, Ruta de Carlos V, and the Vía Verde Santander-Mediterráneo, among others.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a challenging road cycling trip like this?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, it's essential to bring appropriate gear. This includes a well-maintained road bike, helmet, sufficient water and nutrition, repair kit, and clothing suitable for varying weather conditions. Consider layers, as the 'broken and isolated landscape' can lead to temperature changes.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through charming villages that have maintained their traditional architecture. While specific cafes are not highlighted, these villages may offer opportunities for refreshments. It's always a good idea to carry enough water and snacks, especially on a challenging route in a somewhat isolated area.
